findings.description: Stored XSS: display_name, action, created_at and title are interpolated into HTML with no escaping, so user-controlled DB values containing markup execute script in the admin's browser.
Per-row f-string interpolation of event['display_name'] and event['action'] into <td> cells without html.escape allows persisted script/markup injection into the rendered report.
Contract violation: docstring promises an inclusive [start, end] range but the query uses created_at < end, so events on the end day with any time component are silently excluded.
save_report writes to an unvalidated out_path, permitting path traversal / arbitrary file overwrite if the path is attacker- or user-influenced.
No error handling around sqlite connect/execute; OperationalError (db locked, missing table, disk full) propagates uncaught with no graceful degradation, contrary to failure-handling expectations.
limit is passed straight to SQL LIMIT with no bound check; a negative limit in SQLite means unlimited, returning all rows instead of none.
No validation that start <= end across the query methods; an inverted range silently yields empty results / zero counts rather than signalling caller misuse.
save_report does not create the parent directory or handle OSError, so writing to a non-existent directory raises an uncaught exception.
